finding.title: Duplicate id '{group}' in {count} files
finding.explanation: The id '{group}' is declared by multiple archives: {values}. Only one can load.

finding.title: Known-incompatible mods both installed: {r.subject} + {r.related}
finding.explanation: `{r.subject}` is known to be incompatible with `{r.related}`, and both are installed. {r.reason}
finding.fix: Remove one of `{r.subject}` or `{r.related}` — they cannot run together.

rules.where: m.subject IS NOT NULL AND e.loader IN ('fabric', 'quilt', 'forge', 'neoforge') AND m.loader IN ('fabric', 'quilt', 'forge', 'neoforge') AND m.loader != e.loader

finding.title: '{m.subject}' is a {m.loader} mod on a {e.loader} instance
finding.explanation: {m.subject} is built for {m.loader}, but this instance runs {e.loader}. It will not load.
finding.fix: Use the {e.loader} build of this mod, or remove it.

rules.where: m.side IS NOT NULL AND m.side != 'both' AND e.side = 'server' AND m.side = 'client'

finding.title: '{m.subject}' is client-only on a server
finding.explanation: {m.subject} declares environment=client; on a dedicated server it is dead weight or may error.

rules.where: m.side IS NOT NULL AND m.side != 'both' AND e.side = 'client' AND m.side = 'server'

finding.title: '{m.subject}' is server-only on a client
finding.explanation: {m.subject} declares environment=server; it will do nothing client-side.

finding.title: Resource can be merged safely: {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) touch this path: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: Generate an overlay preview and inspect the merged tag values.

finding.title: JSON resource needs merge review: {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) touch this path: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: Review the JSON files; InterMed can stage a deterministic overlay preview.

finding.title: Resource override is order-dependent: {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) touch this path: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: Choose the intended winner or add a compatibility datapack/resourcepack overlay.

finding.title: Tag merge is order-dependent (replace): {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) touch this tag: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: A writer uses "replace": true. Confirm the intended winner; the set union is not order-independent.

finding.title: Tag entries carry required flags: {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) touch this tag: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: Review the optional/required entries; values still union but semantics can differ.

finding.title: Invalid tag document: {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) touch this tag path: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: Inspect the malformed tag JSON; it cannot be merged as a tag.

finding.title: Single-document JSON override: {subject}
finding.explanation: {writer_count} writer(s) define this {attr:domain}: {writers_list}. {attr:reason}
finding.fix: Only one definition survives by load order. Choose the intended winner or rename one.

finding.title: Mixin target overlap: {subject}
finding.explanation: Multiple mods target {subject}: {attr:mods}. Operations: {attr:operations}.
finding.fix: Check mod compatibility notes and prefer versions known to share this target.

finding.title: Mixin target overlap: {subject}
finding.explanation: Multiple mods target {subject}: {attr:mods}. Operations: {attr:operations}.
finding.fix: Check mod compatibility notes and prefer versions known to share this target.

finding.title: High-risk @Overwrite mixin: {attr:target}
finding.explanation: {subject} overwrites code in {attr:target}. @Overwrite has a high compatibility risk because it replaces target behavior.
finding.fix: Prefer versions without competing overwrites, or remove one conflicting mod.

finding.title: High-risk @Overwrite mixin: {attr:target}
finding.explanation: {subject} overwrites code in {attr:target}. @Overwrite has a high compatibility risk because it replaces target behavior.
finding.fix: Prefer versions without competing overwrites, or remove one conflicting mod.

finding.title: Unknown mod provenance: {subject}
finding.explanation: This jar has no recognizable Fabric/Quilt/Forge manifest. Provenance and supply-chain trust cannot be established.
finding.fix: Prefer mods from Modrinth or CurseForge with verifiable metadata.

finding.title: Jar is not JAR-signed: {subject}
finding.explanation: No META-INF/*.SF signature block was found. Most Fabric/Forge mods ship unsigned; this is informational only.
finding.fix: Verify mod source manually if supply-chain trust matters.

finding.title: Low-provenance jar `{subject}` exercises high-risk capability
finding.explanation: `{subject}` could not be confidently identified (trust score {trust}/100) yet statically references high-risk capability/capabilities: {capabilities}. Unknown provenance combined with a dangerous capability is a stronger supply-chain concern than either signal alone.
finding.fix: Establish the mod's provenance (known platform + signed/manifest) before trusting a jar that spawns processes, loads native code, or evaluates scripts.

finding.title: Mod ships a Forge coremod: {subject}
finding.explanation: `{subject}` declares the coremod `{attr:name}` — a JavaScript bytecode-manipulation script that rewrites classes at load time, outside the loader's mixin/compatibility machinery. Coremods bypass conflict detection and break often across Minecraft/Forge updates, so review this mod first when triaging hard crashes or class-loading errors.
finding.fix: Prefer mixins or access transformers over coremods where possible, and check for an updated build of this mod after any Minecraft/Forge upgrade.
